title: Acwa wins dual awards at 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid
content:
Acwa won Desalination Company of the Year and Desalination Plant of the Year at the 2026 Global Water Awards ceremony in Madrid on June 3, the company announced. The recognition marks the second time Acwa has claimed the top awards from Global Water Intelligence, which has recognized excellence in the water sector since 2006.

Acwa manages 9.7 million cubic meters per day of desalination capacity globally, positioning itself as the world's largest private water desalination company. The awards acknowledge the company's operational performance and delivery of water solutions across multiple markets.

The company's Rabigh 3 Independent Water Project, a 600,000 cubic meter per day seawater reverse osmosis plant serving the Makkah region, achieved availability levels above 99 percent and recently completed a refinancing aligned with IFC Blue Finance Guidelines.

Dr. Tariq Nada, head of technical services for Acwa's global water division, said the awards reflected "the engineers, operators and partners across our entire ecosystem" who built the desalination platform. "We are proud to provide a significant share of Saudi Arabia's desalinated water capacity," he said in a statement.

Yasir Mahmoud, executive vice president of global water at Acwa, attributed the recognition to the company's operational model. "Being named Desalination Company of the Year, alongside Desalination Plant of the Year, is the clearest signal yet that our model works — lower energy intensity, higher availability and smarter financing," he said.

The Global Water Awards recognize projects, companies and individuals across 15 categories that reshape how the world manages water, from desalination and wastewater treatment to digital innovation and sustainable financing.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-recognized-as-a-global-leader-in-water-at-the-2026-global-water-awards-in-madrid/

title: Acwa Power Signs 500-MW Solar-Storage Deal in Philippines
content:
Acwa Power Philippines signed a contract of lease with the Bases Conversion and Development Authority for a 500-hectare site in New Clark City to develop an integrated solar photovoltaic and battery energy storage facility, the company announced on June 2.

The project represents an initial investment of at least USD 400,000 per megawatt, with a capacity of up to 500 MW upon full build-out, subject to final design and regulatory approvals. The facility will supply power to data centers and advanced industries within the Philippines' flagship smart city.

Finance Secretary Frederick D. Go said the investment "lays the foundation upon which semiconductors, advanced manufacturing, and AI-driven industries can be built at utility scale." He added the project makes New Clark City "one of the strongest investment destinations in the region, while creating meaningful, lasting jobs for Filipinos."

Acwa Power Chief Executive Officer Dr. Samir J Serhan stated that the partnership reflects investor confidence in the Philippines for large-scale clean infrastructure. "New Clark City reflects the kind of integrated, future-ready development that global energy investors are looking for," he said.

The project is among key investment commitments secured by the Philippine government under President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. in support of sustainable economic growth and energy security objectives.

Salman M. Baray, Country General Manager of Acwa Philippines, said the 500-hectare footprint allows the company to build an integrated rather than incremental project. Acwa Power is a subsidiary of Saudi-listed Acwa Power Company and operates across water desalination, energy transition and green hydrogen sectors.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-bcda-advance-philippines-clean-energy-transition-with-500-mw-solar-plus-storage-project-in-new-clark-city/

title: Acwa Power, Emerging Power ink deal for 5,000 MW Philippines clean energy push
content:
Acwa Power and Emerging Power Inc. signed a joint development agreement to pursue up to 5,000 MW of renewable energy and gas-fired generation capacity across the Philippines by 2030, the companies said on June 2.

Emerging Power, the clean energy arm of nickel producer Nickel Asia Corporation, will partner with Acwa Power Philippines, a subsidiary of Saudi-listed Acwa, to identify and co-develop new power projects nationwide. A priority near-term track is joint participation in the Philippines' Green Energy Auction Program, where the partners plan to submit competitive proposals.

The Philippines has set ambitious clean energy targets in Southeast Asia, with rapid electrification and growing data center demand pressuring the power sector. Acwa, a leading private water desalination company and energy transition player, brings three decades of experience delivering large-scale power and water assets. Nickel Asia brings deep local knowledge and an operating renewables base through Emerging Power.

"The Philippines has set one of the most ambitious clean energy trajectories in Southeast Asia, and reaching it requires partners who can move at scale and at pace," said Samir J Serhan, Chief Executive Officer of Acwa.

Salman M. Baray, Country General Manager of Acwa Philippines, said the agreement represents "the collaboration we've been building toward since we established our presence in the Philippines last year." He added that the Green Energy Auction Program is "the obvious first test, but the real value is the longer pipeline we can shape together."

Acwa established its Philippine operations in 2025 and has been expanding its portfolio in the region's energy transition.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-and-emerging-power-inc-sign-joint-development-agreement-jda-to-build-up-to-5-000-mw-clean-energy-platform-by-2030-in-the-philippines/

title: EDF, Masdar Sign 15-Year Power Deal for California Solar Project
content:
EDF power solutions North America and Masdar have signed 15-year power purchase agreements for the BigBeau Solar+Storage Project in Kern County, California. The facility, which became operational in December 2022, began delivering electricity to Southern California Edison on February 1, 2026.

The project comprises a 128 megawatt solar plant and a 40 MW/160 MWh battery energy storage system. The two companies are co-owners of BigBeau, one of seven projects they agreed to develop together with a combined capacity of 1.1 gigawatts.

Dustin Priemer, Director Asset Management Masdar Americas, said the agreement reflects the company's focus on scaling reliable utility-scale clean power. "We are appreciative of our growing partnership with Southern California Edison and our shared commitment to investing in new generation capacity to meet growing energy demand in California," he said.

Jacqueline de Fresart, Associate Director of Origination and Power Marketing at EDF power solutions North America, added that the company was pleased to support Edison's clean energy goals. "We are excited to partner with SCE again and look forward to more opportunities together," she said.

EDF power solutions has developed 26 gigawatts of wind, solar and storage projects across North America with over 35 years of experience. Masdar has invested several billion dollars in the United States since 2019 and plans to develop a portfolio of up to 25 gigawatts within the next decade.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/edf-power-solutions-north-america-and-masdar-sign-agreements-for-solar-and-energy-storage-project

title: RWE, Masdar get approval for 3GW Dogger Bank South wind farms
content:
RWE and Masdar have received planning permission for two 1.5GW offshore wind farms off the northeast coast of England, with a combined capacity of 3GW.

Lord Whitehead, acting on behalf of the UK Energy Secretary, approved the Development Consent Order for Dogger Bank South West and Dogger Bank South East. The projects, located over 100km off the Yorkshire coast, are being developed as a joint venture with RWE holding 51% and Masdar holding 49%.

The wind farms are expected to generate enough electricity annually to power approximately three million UK homes. Both projects secured Contracts for Difference through the UK Government's Allocation Round 7 in January 2026.

The planning application was originally submitted in June 2024, with the Planning Inspectorate reviewing over 1,000 documents and conducting 10 online examination hearings.

RWE and Masdar will now undertake detailed final design work and procurement decisions, with the aim of reaching a final investment decision in 2027. The projects have potential to create business and employment opportunities in Yorkshire, the Humber region, and across the UK.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/rwe-and-masdar-receive-planning-permission-for-3gw-dogger-bank-south-offshore-wind-farm-projects

title: RWE wins £91.20/MWh contracts for 6.9GW UK offshore wind
content:
RWE has secured contracts for difference for 6.9 gigawatts of offshore wind capacity in the UK government's latest renewable energy auction, at a strike price of £91.20 per megawatt hour in 2024 prices.

The German utility won 20-year contracts in Allocation Round 7 for four projects in the British North Sea and one in the Irish Sea. These include Norfolk Vanguard East and West, two Dogger Bank South developments, and Awel y Môr, according to the UK Department for Energy Security and Net Zero.

RWE is partnering with investment firm KKR to develop and operate Norfolk Vanguard East and West. KKR will acquire a 50% equity stake in each project, with both companies jointly developing, constructing and operating the wind farms. The transaction is expected to close in summer 2026, subject to customary approvals.

Markus Krebber, chief executive of RWE AG, said the company was "delighted" to secure long-term contracts and to partner with KKR. "By combining KKR's investment know-how in large-scale, complex infrastructure projects with RWE's extensive offshore wind expertise, we are well positioned to jointly realise these major projects," he said.

RWE is also developing the Dogger Bank South projects with Masdar and the Awel y Môr project with Stadtwerke München and Siemens.

The contracts represent a key milestone for RWE's UK offshore wind pipeline as the company executes its renewable energy expansion strategy.

Source: https://uk.rwe.com/press-and-news/2026-01-14-rwe-secures-contracts-for-difference-for-6-9-gigawatts-of-offshore-wind-capacity/

title: EWEC, Masdar sign deal to deploy 30GW solar, 8GW battery storage
content:
Abu Dhabi's Emirates Water and Electricity Company and Masdar have signed a strategic collaboration framework to accelerate deployment of more than 30GW of solar PV capacity and over 8GW of battery energy storage across the United Arab Emirates.

The Collaboration Framework Agreement, signed by EWEC Chief Executive Officer Ahmed Ali Alshamsi and Masdar Chief Executive Officer Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, establishes a roadmap for utility-scale renewable energy projects designed to diversify the UAE's energy mix and strengthen supply security while supporting local industrial growth.

The agreement will streamline Masdar's participation in EWEC's engagement process for renewable energy projects, covering early-stage development through to financial close. It builds on previous partnerships including the Al Dhafra Solar PV, Al Ajban Solar PV, and Khazna Solar PV projects.

The framework aims to improve efficiency while maintaining transparency and independence in EWEC's competitive procurement processes and maximizing in-country value and Emirati talent development.

"This Collaboration Framework Agreement with Masdar is a pivotal strategic tool that empowers us to accelerate this transformation and meet 60 per cent of Abu Dhabi's total energy demand from renewable and clean sources by 2035," Alshamsi said in a statement.

EWEC operates integrated planning, purchasing, supply and system despatch services for water and electricity across the UAE. The deployment targets support Abu Dhabi's renewable and clean energy objectives while reshaping the energy sector's cost structures and operational efficiency.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/wec-and-masdar-forge-strategic-framework-to-scale-renewable-energy

title: Masdar marks 20 years in global clean energy development
content:
Abu Dhabi's renewable energy company Masdar is marking two decades of operations across clean energy projects worldwide.

The company was founded in 2006 as an Abu Dhabi government-backed renewable energy developer. Masdar has expanded its portfolio to include solar, wind and other clean energy initiatives across multiple continents.

The milestone reflects the broader energy transition underway globally as countries seek to reduce carbon emissions and shift away from fossil fuels. Masdar's two-decade trajectory mirrors increased investment in renewable energy infrastructure over the past 20 years, driven by falling technology costs and climate policy support.

The company has positioned itself as a major player in the global clean energy sector through acquisitions, partnerships and direct project development. Masdar operates renewable energy assets in Europe, the Middle East, Africa and Asia-Pacific regions.

The anniversary comes as the renewable energy sector continues expanding. Global renewable capacity additions have accelerated in recent years, with solar and wind leading growth. Masdar's longevity in the sector reflects sustained demand for clean energy solutions from both government and corporate buyers worldwide.

The company's two-decade presence underscores the maturation of renewable energy as a significant part of the global energy mix, moving beyond early-stage technology demonstration to mainstream commercial deployment.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/masdar-20-years-of-powering-the-worlds-clean-energy-future

title: Masdar and EPCG form joint venture for renewable energy in Montenegro
content:
Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company PJSC (Masdar) and Elektroprivreda Crne Gore (EPCG), Montenegro's national power utility, have agreed to establish a 50/50 joint venture to develop large-scale renewable energy projects in Montenegro.

The agreement was signed at the Adria Future Summit in the presence of Montenegro's Minister of Energy and Mining Admir Šahmanović and UAE Ambassador to Montenegro Khamis Rashed Ahmed Lebsaili Alshemeili. Masdar CEO Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, EPCG President Milutin Đukanović and EPCG CEO Zdravko Dragaš signed the accord.

The joint venture, to be headquartered in Nikšić, will deploy and operate clean energy projects across solar photovoltaic, wind, hydropower, pumped hydro energy storage, stand-alone battery energy storage systems and hybrid solutions in Montenegro.

The partnership aims to support Montenegro's domestic energy needs while enabling export of renewable electricity to the Western Balkans and Southern Europe, including through an existing sub-sea interconnection with Italy.

Šahmanović said the partnership represents a turning point for Montenegro's energy future. "By joining forces, Masdar and EPCG bring together global knowledge, strong national capacity and a shared ambition to speed up the transition towards clean energy," he said.

The collaboration builds on Masdar's existing presence in Montenegro and complements its expansion across the wider region. The joint venture combines EPCG's position as Montenegro's leading energy producer with Masdar's global renewable energy project development expertise.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/masdar-and-epcg-to-form-joint-venture

title: ScottishPower, Masdar install record 115-metre blades at UK offshore wind farm
content:
ScottishPower and Masdar have installed the first turbine at East Anglia THREE offshore windfarm off the Suffolk coast, setting a UK record with 115-metre blades, the longest manufactured and used in the country.

The 14MW Siemens Gamesa turbines stand approximately 262 metres tall with a rotor diameter of 236 metres. All 285 blades for the project's 95 turbines are manufactured at Siemens Gamesa's factory in Hull.

The £4 billion East Anglia THREE project will have a capacity of 1.4 GW when complete, generating enough electricity to power more than 1.3 million homes. A single turbine revolution produces enough power to supply a UK home for more than four days.

Charlie Jordan, ScottishPower Renewables CEO, said the installation represents "a defining moment and a UK industry first" for the partners. "East Anglia THREE will be the biggest and most powerful offshore windfarm in our portfolio. That means billions of pounds invested in UK and global supply chains; thousands of jobs supported during construction; more than a hundred long-term roles created in the East of England," Jordan said.

Husain Al Meer, Director of Global Offshore Wind at Masdar, called the turbine installation "a milestone moment for the UK's offshore wind sector."

East Anglia THREE will rank among the world's largest offshore windfarms upon completion, delivering homegrown clean electricity to support the UK's energy security and climate goals.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/first-turbine-installed-at-east-anglia-three

title: WiSER platform supports women leaders in sustainability transition
content:
WiSER, a global platform established in 2015 by Masdar and the Zayed Sustainability Prize, has trained 150 young women from 30+ nationalities through its flagship Pioneers Program since launch.

The platform operates across three pillars: education, engagement and empowerment. The year-long WiSER Pioneers Program targets women aged 25-35, offering educational workshops, mentoring and networking opportunities with industry experts to prepare them for roles in the net-zero energy transition.

WiSER has provided 565+ hours of mentoring to young women sustainability leaders and conducted 540+ hours of workshops with program participants. The platform has convened 4,000+ influential figures to advance gender parity and sustainability.

The organization operates three core programs. Beyond the Pioneers Program, WiSER Cares combines cultural exchange with hands-on learning across five pillars: leadership, innovation, energy nexus, community engagement and global network. A dedicated mentorship program pairs Pioneers with senior industry mentors to support career growth and leadership development.

WiSER is rooted in recognition that women, often disproportionately affected by environmental and development challenges, can serve as powerful agents of change. The platform is guided by the United Arab Emirates' sustainability vision and the legacy of Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan.

The organization hosts an annual WiSER Forum focused on igniting a global sustainable economy.

Source: https://wiser.ae/

title: TotalEnergies and Masdar form $2.2bn Asia renewable joint venture
content:
TotalEnergies and Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company PJSC, known as Masdar, have signed a binding agreement to establish a $2.2 billion 50/50 joint venture merging their onshore renewable activities across nine Asian countries.

The joint venture will operate in Azerbaijan, Indonesia, Japan, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea and Uzbekistan. It will develop, build, own and operate onshore solar, wind and battery storage projects as the sole vehicle for both companies' renewable energy operations in these markets.

The venture will initially manage a portfolio of 3 GW of operational capacity and 6 GW of assets under advanced development expected to become operational by 2030. Each partner will contribute assets of comparable value to the partnership.

"Asia will be the main driver of global electricity demand growth this decade, and this collaboration with TotalEnergies will accelerate our progress across the continent," said His Excellency Dr. Sultan Al Jaber, UAE Minister of Industry and Advanced Technology and Chairman of Masdar.

TotalEnergies has previously developed renewable energy projects in Central Asia and the Caucasus region. The partnership positions both companies to scale renewable energy deployment across Asia's most attractive growth markets.

The transaction remains subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals. Financial terms indicate each company contributes assets of equivalent value to the $2.2 billion joint venture structure.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/totalenergies-and-masdar

title: Exus to acquire 60% stake in Masdar's Portuguese wind portfolio
content:
Exus Renewables has agreed to acquire a 60 percent stake in a portfolio of nine wind farms in Portugal from Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company PJC, known as Masdar, which will retain a 40 percent stake.

The wind farms are located in Guarda and Castelo Branco in northern and central Portugal. Masdar is currently repowering the portfolio, which will increase total operational capacity from 144MW to 164MW. The repowering projects are in their final development phase, with completion expected by 2027.

Once fully operational, the repowered sites will generate electricity for more than 200,000 households and avoid approximately 41.7 kilotons of CO2 emissions annually.

Luis Adão da Fonseca, CEO at Exus Renewables, said the deal "significantly strengthens Exus' operational presence across Europe and underscores our innovative approach to maximising the performance and value of renewable assets."

Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, Chief Executive Officer of Masdar, said the companies are "enhancing the performance and long-term value of these sites" through repowering and hybridization.

The portfolio also includes plans for hybridization with 110MW of additional solar capacity to be codeveloped by both companies. This would further enhance the portfolio's capacity and contribute to Portugal's national clean energy targets.

Portugal's energy transition roadmap targets 10.4 GW of onshore wind capacity by 2030 and achieving net zero emissions by 2050.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/exus-renewables-to-acquire-stake-in-masdars-portuguese-wind-portfolio

title: Masdar expanding clean energy push in Uzbekistan with $2bn investment
content:
Masdar has committed over $2 billion to renewable energy projects in Uzbekistan since entering the country in 2018, according to the latest episode of the company's Power to Change documentary series released on November 13.

The investments span major solar and wind developments, including the 500MW Zarafshan Wind Farm, the 457MW Sherabad solar project and the 100MW Nur Navoi solar facility. The Abu Dhabi-based renewable energy company is now advancing Uzbekistan's first standalone Battery Energy Storage System, a development intended to stabilize grid supply and ensure reliable power generation around the clock.

Ilholm Jon Teshabaev, General Director of Uzbekistan Masdar, said the country has transitioned from its industrial past toward cleaner energy. "The world is moving forward and so have we," he said.

Abdulla Zayed, Director of Development and Investment at Masdar, described the company's role in supporting the country's energy transition. "From the beginning, our mission was to identify how Masdar could best support Uzbekistan's transition to a green and more sustainable energy system," Zayed said.

The documentary highlights how Masdar's developments are delivering secure, affordable energy to communities across Central Asia's most ambitious renewable energy program. The company's expansion underscores growing international cooperation in accelerating clean energy progress in the region.

Source: https://masdar.ae/news/newsroom?topicID={AF1B6ECC-3E90-4CAF-A036-6C76456D4A95}

title: Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week draws record 60,000 attendees, 15 heads of state
content:
Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week 2026 concluded as its largest edition, drawing over 60,000 attendees and convening 15 heads of state and government alongside more than 400 ministers and government officials to address global sustainability challenges.

Held January 11-15 under the theme "The Nexus of Next: All Systems Go," the event was hosted by Masdar and held under the patronage of UAE President His Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. Attendance increased 20% compared to the previous edition, with participants representing leaders from energy, finance, food, water and nature sectors.

The week featured more than 240 dedicated sessions with over 800 speakers addressing global energy systems transformation, food and water security, and nature conservation. The turnout marked a significant increase from the previous year, when the event hosted approximately 200 ministers and government officials.

Masdar positioned the gathering as the world's largest sustainability event, using the five-day forum to drive dialogue on interconnected global systems and their potential for scaled solutions. Organizers said the expanded participation reflected growing momentum around sustainability initiatives and the UAE's clean energy leadership on the global stage.

The event highlighted commitments from participating nations to accelerate real-world climate solutions and systems transformation across multiple sectors. Additional details on specific outcomes and agreements reached during the week were not disclosed in available information.

Source: https://masdar.ae/news/newsroom
